Honey never spoils. Archaeologists found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible.
The first computer bug was an actual bug — a moth stuck in a Harvard Mark II relay in 1947.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
The entire internet weighs about the same as a strawberry — roughly 50 grams of electrons.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
The first 1GB hard drive (1980) weighed 550 pounds and cost $40,000.
Bananas are naturally radioactive due to their potassium content.
Git was created by Linus Torvalds in just 10 days.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes — between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896.
NASA's Apollo 11 computer had less processing power than a modern calculator.
Venus is the only planet that spins clockwise.
The @ symbol was nearly extinct before Ray Tomlinson used it for email in 1971.
A teaspoon of a neutron star would weigh about 6 billion tons.
The first website ever made is still online: info.cern.ch.
Cows have best friends and get stressed when separated.
Linux runs on over 96% of the world's top 1 million servers.
The human brain uses about 20 watts of power — less than a dim light bulb.
There are more trees on Earth (~3 trillion) than stars in the Milky Way (~400 billion).
The word 'robot' comes from Czech 'robota', meaning forced labor.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
The first message sent over the internet was 'LO' — it crashed before completing 'LOGIN'.
Sharks are older than trees. Sharks: ~400 million years. Trees: ~350 million years.
The Unicode standard contains over 149,000 characters covering 161 scripts.
Wombat poop is cube-shaped to prevent it from rolling away.
Python is named after Monty Python, not the snake.
Water can boil and freeze at the same time — it's called the 'triple point'.
The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, in the 1840s.
An average cumulus cloud weighs about 1.1 million pounds.
JavaScript was created in 10 days by Brendan Eich in 1995.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the building of the Great Pyramid.
The inventor of the Pringles can is buried in one.
There are 10 times more bacteria in your body than human cells.
The spacebar is the most commonly pressed key on a keyboard.
Scotland's national animal is the unicorn.
The first alarm clock could only ring at 4 AM.
A single Google search uses about 0.3 watt-hours of energy.
Tardigrades can survive in the vacuum of space.
The average person walks about 100,000 miles in a lifetime — roughly 4 times around Earth.
The term 'debugging' became popular after Grace Hopper removed that moth from the Harvard Mark II.
Your brain generates enough electricity to power a small LED light.
The first YouTube video was uploaded on April 23, 2005 — 'Me at the zoo'.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The entire Wikipedia database is about 22 GB compressed.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the sun.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft runs on about 70 KB of memory.
Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas.
The 'save' icon is a floppy disk — most people under 20 have never seen one.
The longest hiccuping spree lasted 68 years.
Stack Overflow was launched in 2008 and has over 50 million questions.
